name: Aldeanueva de Barbarroya and Vía Verde de la Jara Loop
name_original: Aldeanueva de Barbarroya a Belvís de la Jara a Buenasbodas — circular por la Vía Verde de la Jara
description: Explore the Aldeanueva de Barbarroya and Vía Verde de la Jara Loop, a moderate 31.8-mile mountain biking route with diverse scenery.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya are set within the diverse natural landscapes of the La Jara region in Toledo, Spain. The area features tranquil riverbanks, sierras, and Mediterranean woodlands known as dehesas. A significant feature for mountain biking is the Vía Verde de la Jara, a converted railway line offering accessible routes through the Tagus River valley. This terrain provides varied options for exploring the region's natural beauty. Best mountain bike trails around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya The most popular mountain bike trail is Aldeanueva de Barbarroya a Belvís de la Jara a Buenasbodas — circular por la Vía Verde de la Jara, a 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route follows sections of the Vía Verde de la Jara, offering views of the Tagus River valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puerto de San Vicente a Antigua estación Santa Quiteria — circular por La Estrella desde Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé, a moderate 37.0 miles (59.5 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including natural elevations and Mediterranean forests. Local mountain bikers also love the Vía Verde de la Jara – circular al embalse de Azután desde Belvís de la Jara, a 22.5 miles (36.2 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde de la Jara and towards the Azután Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Mountain biking around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya is defined by the Vía Verde de la Jara, the Tagus River plain, and extensive dehesas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Aldeanueva De Barbarroya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aldeanueva De Barbarroya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Aldeanueva de Barbarroya?
A: The terrain around Aldeanueva de Barbarroya is diverse, featuring tranquil riverbanks, sierras, and Mediterranean woodlands known as dehesas. Many routes utilize the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346909911/aldeanueva-de-barbarroya-a-belvis-de-la-jara-a-buenasbodas-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, an old disused railway line, which offers gentle slopes and accessible paths. You'll encounter varied surfaces, from smooth greenway sections to more natural trails through forests.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Aldeanueva de Barbarroya?
A: Yes, the region offers options for various skill levels, including easy routes. The Vía Verde de la Jara is particularly well-suited for beginners due to its gentle slopes. There are 2 easy mountain bike tours available, providing a good starting point for less experienced riders.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking more demanding trails, there are 3 difficult mountain bike routes available around Aldeanueva de Barbarroya. These routes will offer more technical sections and greater elevation changes compared to the easier options.

Q: What natural features or attractions can I see while mountain biking in Aldeanueva de Barbarroya?
A: You can explore a variety of natural and historical attractions. Many routes offer views of the Tagus River and the Azután Reservoir. Notable points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3851007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amador Bridge or Azután Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, which provides spectacular views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Azután Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The trails also traverse Mediterranean forests and dehesas, offering scenic natural backdrops.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Tagus River or Azután Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The region is situated on the fertile plain of the Tagus River, and many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023054626/via-verde-de-la-jara-circular-al-embalse-de-azutan-desde-belvis-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara – circular al embalse de Azután desde Belvís de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, lead directly to or offer panoramic views of the Azután Reservoir and the Tagus River valley.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346909911/aldeanueva-de-barbarroya-a-belvis-de-la-jara-a-buenasbodas-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeanueva de Barbarroya a Belvís de la Jara a Buenasbodas — circular por la Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive loop through the area's highlights.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around Aldeanueva de Barbarroya suitable for families?
A: The Vía Verde de la Jara, with its gentle slopes and accessible nature, is particularly suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly categorized, the easier trails provide a safe and enjoyable experience for riders of all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Aldeanueva de Barbarroya?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if riding during this season. Winters are typically mild.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Aldeanueva de Barbarroya?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the tranquility of the riverbanks and dehesas, and the accessibility of routes like the Vía Verde de la Jara.

Q: Is there public transport access to the mountain bike trails?
A: While Aldeanueva de Barbarroya is a rural area, public transport options might be limited directly to trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to the main town. Many visitors prefer to arrive by car, which offers more flexibility for accessing various starting points.

Q: Are there places to park near the mountain bike trailheads?
A: Parking availability can vary depending on the specific trailhead. In Aldeanueva de Barbarroya and surrounding villages, you can typically find parking in or near the town centers, which often serve as starting points for routes. For routes along the Vía Verde de la Jara, designated parking areas may be available at former station sites or access points.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Mediterranean forests or dehesas?
A: Yes, the landscape is rich with Mediterranean forests and dehesas, and many routes traverse these scenic areas. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350476821/puerto-de-san-vicente-a-antigua-estacion-santa-quiteria-circular-por-la-estrella-desde-aldeanueva-de-san-bartolome" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de San Vicente a Antigua estación Santa Quiteria — circular por La Estrella desde Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé&lt;/a&gt; is a route that takes you through varied terrain, including these characteristic woodlands.
tips: Aldeanueva de Barbarroya, in the La Jara region (Toledo), is a small town with a wealth of natural, historical, and cultural treasures. It is especially known for its location on the La Jara Greenway and for being a stopover point on the GR-113 Tajo Nature Trail.

"Belvís" means "beautiful view" perhaps referring to the beautiful landscape that dominates from its hills. The most interesting building is the Iglesia Parroquial de San Andrés Apostol, in Mudejar style.

The Jara Green Route is a 52 km route that follows the old railway line between Calera y Chozas and Santa Quiteria. This route is ideal for cyclists and hikers, as it crosses unspoiled landscapes and has 18 tunnels and 6 viaducts, offering spectacular views of the Tagus River and the surrounding valleys.

The route begins at Calera y Chozas station and passes through several historic stations, including Aldeanueva de Barbarroya. It is a perfect destination to enjoy nature and tranquility, with options to explore it on foot, by bicycle or on horseback.

faqs.question: What is the current status of the Vía Verde de la Jara section of this loop?
faqs.answer: It is crucial to know that the Vía Verde de la Jara is currently subject to a temporary closure due to significant safety concerns. These include poor surface conditions, a lack of lighting in tunnels, and landslides. Circulation is not permitted, and individuals who disregard this warning do so at their own responsibility. Always check the official status before planning your trip.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The complete Aldeanueva de Barbarroya and Vía Verde de la Jara Loop is rated as moderate for mountain biking. While the Vía Verde de la Jara portion itself is generally easy with minimal inclines, the full loop involves moderate climbs and covers approximately 51.2 kilometers (32 miles) with about 780 meters (2,559 feet) of elevation gain. It requires a good level of physical fitness. However, please remember the Vía Verde de la Jara is currently closed.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: Assuming the trail were open, the Vía Verde de la Jara section features a compacted earth surface, with some asphalted parts, generally suitable for mountain bikes. The broader loop incorporates a diverse mix of paths, dedicated bike paths, and some sections on roads and national roads. The geological landscape transitions from granite to slate formations.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route is known for its 'virgin landscapes' and natural beauty, passing through Mediterranean forests. Highlights include traversing six viaducts and 18 tunnels, some quite long, and the impressive Puente Amador (Viaducto de Azután) over the Tajo River. You'll also pass by several abandoned train stations. The route also goes through Aldeanueva de Barbarroya and near the Parish Church of San Andrés Apóstol, Belvís de la Jara.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: If the trail were open, early spring is particularly beautiful due to the vibrant display of 'jara' (rock rose) flowers. The remote nature of the trail allows for an immersive experience outside of peak times. Always consider weather conditions and the current closure status before planning.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the loop starts and ends in Aldeanueva de Barbarroya, suggesting parking would be available within or near the settlement. Always check local regulations and available parking options before your visit.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde de la Jara is currently closed, generally, dogs are permitted on many Vías Verdes in Spain, often on a leash. However, given the current safety concerns and closure, it is not advisable to bring dogs onto the trail. Always check local signage and regulations for specific rules regarding pets when the trail reopens.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are typically required to access the Vía Verde de la Jara or the surrounding public paths that make up this loop. However, it is essential to respect private property and adhere to any local regulations. Remember, the Vía Verde de la Jara is currently closed to the public.

faqs.question: Does this loop intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it includes significant portions of the Camino de la Jara - Etapa 2 and the Vía Verde de la Jara. It also briefly touches upon the Ruta Mirador Bellavista, Camino Natural del Tajo, and Camino del Valle de Santa María.

faqs.question: What makes this route unique for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for its blend of natural beauty and historical infrastructure. It's built upon a railway line that was never fully operational, featuring numerous tunnels and viaducts that are engineering marvels. The remote and solitary nature of the trail, combined with the chance to see diverse wildlife and the vibrant jara flowers, offers an immersive experience. However, its unique features are currently inaccessible due to the temporary closure.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The Mediterranean forests along the route are home to diverse flora and fauna. If the trail were open, you might spot deer, rabbits, and various birds of prey, offering a rich wildlife viewing experience.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of San Andrés Apóstol, Belvís de la Jara
